svg.title: fak — the agent kernel, vs the serving stacks (capability matrix)
svg.desc: A capability matrix: capabilities grouped by SERVING REUSE, CORRECTNESS, SECURITY KERNEL and a SINGLE-STREAM fence down the left; fak, vLLM, SGLang, llama.cpp and an API prompt cache across the top, fak highlighted. fak carries a value in every row; the serving stacks carry an em-dash outside the serving and single-stream bands. Only fak shows measured numbers; the fence inverts the highlight to the SOTA leader.
svg.text: fak spans the whole boundary; the serving stacks span one band of it
Serving engines win raw throughput and front-prefix reuse — and stop there. fak's cache is addressable — it reaches into the middle of a
kept run — so it does that reuse too, then proves it exact and makes the permission gate a syscall. An em-dash means a capability the
stack does not ship, not a measured zero.
the band the SOTA stacks own — fak matches it
keep the run from the first token
SECURITY KERNEL — THE BOUNDARY THEY DON'T OWN
the cost of NOT owning it
shown, not hidden — fak does not target raw throughput
Only the fak column carries measured figures; every one traces to fak/BENCHMARK-AUTHORITY.md by the commit shown at the row's right. Machine: Apple M3 Pro 6P+6E 36 GB,
app_version 0.30.0, llama.cpp b9707 on the same box.
An em-dash (—) marks a capability the stack does not ship — a structural gap, not a measured zero. “n/a” marks a row that does not apply to a hosted API cache.
Serving reuse: 86.7% KV-hit is reported against SGLang RadixAttention's published 50–99% band; 4.1× fleet serving is fak's own kernel held constant (so it isolates
reuse, not kernel speed) — it is a [SOTA-style] result, not a green win. The naive re-prefill comparison (~60×) is deliberately omitted as a strawman.
Correctness: max|Δ|=0 is the L∞ between an evicted-span run and a clean run that never saw the span (TestBatchedDecodeMatchesSerial / mid-run eviction). Greedy parity
is 7/7 exact tokens vs llama.cpp on identical Q8 weights.
Security kernel: 362 ns decide and 3.3 µs admit are in-process p50; 2,849× is in-process vs process-per-decide adjudication (n=100). The detector that flags injections
is ≈100% evadable by design — the floor is the default-deny lock + the quarantine, not the classifier.
Single stream: fak explicitly does not target raw throughput — llama.cpp/Metal leads decode (17.3 vs 8.7 tok/s) and prefill. These rows invert the highlight on purpose:
the SOTA stack owns the bold cell. One chat → use llama.cpp; an agent fleet → use fak.
